exercises.name: Shuttle Run
exercises.description:
Sprint back and forth over a short distance, staying low to the ground. 

This exercise improves speed, agility, and cardiovascular endurance.

exercises.name: Side Crunch
exercises.description:
1)  When performing this exercise, shorten the distance between your ribs cage and your pelvis. 

2) Begin by lying down on your side. 

3) Try to roll your rib cage off of the mat as best you can.

exercises.name: Side Lunge
exercises.description:
1) When performing the side lunge, ensure that one leg is straight and your foot knee and hip are aligned on the side that you step up to. 

2) Also, sit your hips back and keep your bodyweight in your heel on the side that you lunge to.

exercises.name: Side Lunge
exercises.description:
1. Start standing with feet together and hands by your sides. 

2. Step one foot out to the side, and slowly descend towards the floor by pushing your hips backwards.

3.While keeping your knee aligned with your foot and hip, push yourself back up to a standing position. 

4. Repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Side lunge with knee raise
exercises.description: Side lunge with knee raise

exercises.name: Side Lying Bench Oblique Crunch
exercises.description:
Lie on your side with your arm on the ground for support. 

Crunch your torso upwards, bringing your elbow toward your hip, then return to the starting position.

exercises.name: Side Lying Thoracic Rotation
exercises.alternateName: Windmill Open Book, Bow and arrow stretch
exercises.description:
1. When performing this exercise, your lower body will anchor your torso. From there, you're going to rotate your chest open and repeat. 

2. Begin by lying on your side, crossing one leg over the other and bending the upper leg to 90 degrees. 

3. Extend both arms at your side straight away from your body.

4. Start the movement by looking back over your shoulder and rotating your top arm towards your backside. 

5. Gently attempt to bring that arm to the floor, hold momentarily and return to the start position. 

6. Perform desired number of repetitions and repeat on the other side.

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description:
1.Lie down on your side. Stack your feet one on top of the other.

2.Prop yourself up arm straight, raising your hips and resting on your elbows.

3.Hold based on recommended time.

exercises.name: Side Plank Cable Row
exercises.alternateName: Side Hip Thrust SA cable core row
exercises.description:
1.Raise your body off the floor, resting on your forearm and foot, holding one handle in your top hand with your arm straight out in front. 

2.Pull the handle straight into your chest, keeping your body in a straight line and your elbow directly under your shoulder.

3.Return to starting position.

4.Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Side Plank Hip Dip
exercises.description:
1) For this exercise, keep your glutes active and maintain a straight line through your body. 

2) Ensure your elbow is below your shoulder as you get into a side plank position. 

3) From here, drop your hip to the ground. 

4) Then push it up towards the ceiling.

exercises.name: Side Plank Hip Lift to Thread the Needle
exercises.description:
Start in a side plank position with your forearm on the floor, elbow under the shoulder, and body in a straight line. 

Engage your core and lower and lift your hips toward the floor for a side plank hip lift. 

After completing the lift, thread the top arm under your torso, rotating your upper body toward the floor (thread the needle).

exercises.name: Side Plank on Knee (Forearm)
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your side on the mat, with your body propped up on your elbow and knees bent. 

2. Stack your knees and push your hips up and away from the mat, while driving your hips forward. 

3. Breathe, and hold this position while keeping a tense abdomen. 

4. Repeat on the opposite side.

exercises.name: Side plank w/ ball/ towel adduction
exercises.description: You can use a ball, towel, stuffed animal. Just something that you will need to engage adductors abs pelvic floor muscles. (PFM)

exercises.name: Side plank w/ DB reverse fly + hold
exercises.description: Keep core tight. You can add a pad or towel under your elbow if it hurts. Perform rep count then hold for the same amount of sec. for example: 15 reps then 15 sec hold

exercises.name: Side plank w/ reverse fly + hold 2
exercises.description: You can use a towel or pad under your elbow if needed. Pull core tight. Reverse fly rep is the same time in seconds you hold at the end. For example 15 reps = 15 sec hold at end.

exercises.name: Side Plank with Abduction
exercises.alternateName: Single Leg Forearm Side Plank leg lift
exercises.description:
1.Lie down on your side. Place your feet together on top of each other. Prop yourself up, raising your hips and resting on your elbows.

2.Raise the other arm towards the ceiling, and your one leg up in the air, creating a 45-degree angle.

3. Hold briefly then return to starting position.

4.Repeat.

exercises.name: Side Plank with Dumbbell Hold
exercises.description:
Start in a side plank position on your forearm, with your body in a straight line from head to feet. 

Hold a dumbbell in your top hand, keeping your arm fully extended toward the ceiling. Keep your hips lifted and core engaged, maintaining balance and stability.

exercises.name: Side plank with knee tuck /hip dip
Side Plank with Rear Fly
exercises.description:
Start in a side plank position on your forearm, with your body in a straight line from head to feet and your top hand holding a dumbbell. 

Keep your core tight and hips lifted. With a slight bend in the elbow, lower the dumbbell toward the floor in front of your chest, then lift it back up toward the ceiling, performing a fly motion while maintaining the side plank position.

exercises.name: Side to side squats
exercises.description: Side to side squats

exercises.name: Side-Lying Hip Axial Rotations
exercises.alternateName: side lying single leg hip rotation internal external
exercises.description: Lie on your side and rotate your top leg internally and externally while keeping it slightly lifted.

exercises.name: Side-Lying Hip Circles
exercises.alternateName: side single leg hip controlled rotations mobility
exercises.description: Lie on your side and lift one leg, drawing small circles in the air to activate the hip muscles.

exercises.name: Side-Lying IT Band Stretch
exercises.description:
1. Start by lying on your side on the mat, with your head supported by the elbow. 

2. Bring the foot of your top leg up towards your back, and grab your foot with top side hand. 

3. Cross your bottom foot across the top thigh. 

4. Breathe and relax in this position, and hold until you achieve a gentle stretch.

exercises.name: Single Arm Cable Overhead Tricep Extension
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright facing away from a cable system holding a rope behind your head in one hand with your elbow bent.

2.Straighten your arm up overhead, keeping your shoulder steady.

3.Bend at the elbow, returning the cable to the start position.

4.Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Single Arm Cable Row
exercises.description:
1.Stand upright with one hand holding the cable, making sure the elbow is close to the body. Palm is in a position like creating a fist, facing away from the body.

2.Squeeze and pull the bar into the body, elbows bent.

3. Straighten at the elbows, returning the cable back to starting position.

4. Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Single Arm Fire Hydrant
exercises.description: On all fours, lift one leg bent at 90 degrees out to the side while holding a dumbbell on the same side. Lower and repeat.

exercises.name: Single Arm Neutral Grip Lat Pulldown
exercises.description:
1.Grasp the handle with your arm extended straight overhead, keeping an upright back position.

2.Pull the handle down, elbow towards the body.

3.Straighten your arm, returning the handle to the starting position, going through a full range of motion.

4.Repeat. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Single Arm Racked Alternating Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
Hold a kettlebell or dumbbell in the racked position. 

Perform a forward lunge with one leg, return to standing, and switch legs.

exercises.name: Single Arm Racked Alternating Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
Alternate reverse lunges with the weight held in the racked position.

Focus on a controlled movement and keep your chest lifted.

exercises.name: Single Arm Racked Ipsilateral Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
With the kettlebell or dumbbell in a racked position, step back into a reverse lunge with the same-side leg. 

Lower your body and drive through the front heel to return to standing.
